topic: Experiences & Present Perfect | Expressing Opinions | Future Predictions & Possibilities

monday.question: What does 'experience' mean?
options: Something that happens to you and affects how you think or feel
A plan you make for the future
A question you ask to learn more
A mistake you make and regret
monday.correctAnswer: Something that happens to you and affects how you think or feel

monday.question: What does 'already' mean in a sentence like 'I have already eaten'?
options: Something that will happen soon
Something that happened before now or earlier than expected
Something that has not happened yet
Something that happens regularly
monday.correctAnswer: Something that happened before now or earlier than expected

monday.question: What does 'yet' mean in 'I haven't finished yet'?
options: Something already completed
Something that will never happen
Something expected to happen but that hasn't happened so far
Something that happened a long time ago
monday.correctAnswer: Something expected to happen but that hasn't happened so far

monday.question: What does 'never' mean?
options: Always; at every time
Sometimes; on certain occasions
Not at any time; at no point in one's life
Rarely; almost never
monday.correctAnswer: Not at any time; at no point in one's life

monday.question: What does 'ever' mean in 'Have you ever been to Paris?'
options: Right now or at this moment
At any time in your life (used in questions and negatives)
Very recently; just a moment ago
Many times; on a regular basis
monday.correctAnswer: At any time in your life (used in questions and negatives)

monday.question: What does 'just' mean in 'She has just arrived'?
options: She arrived a long time ago
She is about to arrive
She arrived a very short time ago
She will arrive soon
monday.correctAnswer: She arrived a very short time ago

monday.question: What does 'recently' mean?
options: In the far past
Not long ago; in the near past
At a specific time in history
In the future
monday.correctAnswer: Not long ago; in the near past

monday.question: What does 'so far' mean?
options: At a great distance from here
Until now; up to this point in time
In a very short time
After a long period
monday.correctAnswer: Until now; up to this point in time

monday.question: What does 'up to now' mean?
options: From now on into the future
Until the present moment
A long time from the present
Exactly at this moment
monday.correctAnswer: Until the present moment

monday.question: In the sentence 'Have you ever tried sushi?', what does 'tried' refer to?
options: A future plan
A regular habit
A life experience at some point in the past
A specific past event with a clear time
monday.correctAnswer: A life experience at some point in the past

monday.question: Which sentence uses Present Perfect correctly for a life experience?
options: I have visited Rome last summer.
I visited Rome in my life.
I have never visited Rome.
I was never visit Rome.
monday.correctAnswer: I have never visited Rome.

monday.question: Which sentence uses Simple Past — NOT Present Perfect?
options: She has lived here for years.
She has already seen that film.
She saw that film last Friday.
She has never eaten Thai food.
monday.correctAnswer: She saw that film last Friday.

monday.question: Where is 'already' correctly placed?
options: She has finished already the report.
She already has finished the report.
She has already finished the report.
Already she has finished the report.
monday.correctAnswer: She has already finished the report.

monday.question: Which sentence uses 'yet' correctly?
options: I have yet eaten dinner.
I haven't eaten dinner yet.
I haven't yet eaten already.
Yet I have eaten dinner.
monday.correctAnswer: I haven't eaten dinner yet.

monday.question: What is the main difference between Present Perfect and Simple Past?
options: Present Perfect is used only for future events.
Simple Past requires a specific time; Present Perfect focuses on the experience or current result.
They can always be used interchangeably.
Present Perfect is only used in formal writing.
monday.correctAnswer: Simple Past requires a specific time; Present Perfect focuses on the experience or current result.

monday.question: Complete: 'Have you _____ tried skydiving?'
monday.correctAnswer: ever

monday.question: Complete: 'I haven't read that book _____. I'll start it this weekend.'
monday.correctAnswer: yet

monday.question: Complete: 'She has _____ been to Japan. She went last summer.'
monday.correctAnswer: already

monday.question: Translate to English: 'Você já foi a Nova York?'
monday.correctAnswer: have you ever been to new york?

monday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu ainda não terminei meu trabalho.'
monday.correctAnswer: i haven't finished my work yet

tuesday.question: What does 'opinion' mean?
options: A proven fact about something
A personal view or belief about something
A question you ask someone
A rule that everyone must follow
tuesday.correctAnswer: A personal view or belief about something

tuesday.question: What does 'believe' mean?
options: To know something for certain based on hard evidence
To think that something is true without absolute proof
To change your mind after a discussion
To understand something completely
tuesday.correctAnswer: To think that something is true without absolute proof

tuesday.question: What does 'agree' mean?
options: To have the same view or feeling as someone else
To argue with someone about something
To explain your idea in detail
To listen without responding
tuesday.correctAnswer: To have the same view or feeling as someone else

tuesday.question: What does 'disagree' mean?
options: To have the same opinion as someone
To repeat what someone said
To have a different opinion from someone
To ask someone to explain better
tuesday.correctAnswer: To have a different opinion from someone

tuesday.question: What does 'perspective' mean?
options: A type of question used in formal debates
A formal written opinion published online
A way of thinking about or seeing something based on your experience
A word used only in academic writing
tuesday.correctAnswer: A way of thinking about or seeing something based on your experience

tuesday.question: What does 'argument' mean in a discussion or academic context?
options: A physical fight between two people
A reason or set of reasons given to support a point of view
A question that has no clear answer
A sentence with no main verb
tuesday.correctAnswer: A reason or set of reasons given to support a point of view

tuesday.question: What does 'convince' mean?
options: To agree with someone immediately
To cause someone to believe that something is true
To repeat the same argument several times
To change your own mind about something
tuesday.correctAnswer: To cause someone to believe that something is true

tuesday.question: What does 'viewpoint' mean?
options: The physical place where you stand
A particular attitude or way of thinking about something
A sentence that starts with 'I think'
A type of grammar structure used in debates
tuesday.correctAnswer: A particular attitude or way of thinking about something

tuesday.question: What does 'on the other hand' mean?
options: As a result of the previous idea
In addition to what was already said
Used to introduce a contrasting point of view
Used to give an example
tuesday.correctAnswer: Used to introduce a contrasting point of view

tuesday.question: What does 'point of view' mean?
options: The exact spot where you are looking
A particular way of thinking about a topic or issue
The most important point in an argument
A writing structure used in essays
tuesday.correctAnswer: A particular way of thinking about a topic or issue

tuesday.question: Which phrase is used to introduce a personal opinion?
options: According to the news, ...
In my opinion, ...
It is known that ...
The research shows ...
tuesday.correctAnswer: In my opinion, ...

tuesday.question: Which is the most natural way to agree with someone?
options: I don't think so.
I totally agree with you.
That doesn't make sense.
I'm not sure about that.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I totally agree with you.

tuesday.question: Which is a polite way to disagree?
options: You're completely wrong.
That's a ridiculous idea.
I see your point, but I disagree because...
No, you're mistaken.
tuesday.correctAnswer: I see your point, but I disagree because...

tuesday.question: Which sentence correctly uses an opinion phrase?
options: From my perspective is important to recycle.
From my perspective, it is important to recycle.
From my perspective it important to recycle.
From perspective, it is important to recycle.
tuesday.correctAnswer: From my perspective, it is important to recycle.

tuesday.question: Which of these is NOT an opinion phrase?
options: I believe that...
In my view, ...
According to scientists, ...
From my perspective, ...
tuesday.correctAnswer: According to scientists, ...

tuesday.question: Complete: '_____ my opinion, social media has more benefits than disadvantages.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: In

tuesday.question: Complete: 'I _____ that everyone should have access to free education.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: believe

tuesday.question: Complete: 'That's a good _____, but I see it differently.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: point

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Na minha opinião, é importante aprender um segundo idioma.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: in my opinion, it is important to learn a second language

tuesday.question: Translate to English: 'Eu discordo porque acho que isso não é justo.'
tuesday.correctAnswer: i disagree because i think this is not fair

wednesday.question: What does 'predict' mean?
options: To remember something from the past
To say what you think will happen in the future
To explain why something happened
To describe something that is happening now
wednesday.correctAnswer: To say what you think will happen in the future

wednesday.question: What does 'probably' mean?
options: Definitely; with complete certainty
Never; not at any time
Almost certainly; very likely to happen
Barely; almost impossible
wednesday.correctAnswer: Almost certainly; very likely to happen

wednesday.question: What does 'possibly' mean?
options: Certainly; without any doubt
Perhaps; it might be true or might happen
Rarely; almost never
Always; every single time
wednesday.correctAnswer: Perhaps; it might be true or might happen

wednesday.question: What does 'likely' mean?
options: Impossible; never going to happen
Difficult to understand or predict
Probably going to happen; expected to occur
Already confirmed to happen
wednesday.correctAnswer: Probably going to happen; expected to occur

wednesday.question: What does 'unlikely' mean?
options: Very certain; guaranteed to happen
Not expected to happen; probably will not occur
Happening right now
Completely impossible under any circumstances
wednesday.correctAnswer: Not expected to happen; probably will not occur

wednesday.question: What does 'forecast' mean?
options: A record of past events
An explanation of a current situation
A prediction about what will happen, especially about weather
A rule about what must be done
wednesday.correctAnswer: A prediction about what will happen, especially about weather

wednesday.question: What does 'expect' mean?
options: To hope something happens but know it won't
To think something will happen based on what you know
To plan something for the near future
To remember something that happened in the past
wednesday.correctAnswer: To think something will happen based on what you know

wednesday.question: What does 'anticipate' mean?
options: To think about and prepare for something that will happen
To be surprised by an unexpected event
To react to something after it happens
To ignore something that might happen
wednesday.correctAnswer: To think about and prepare for something that will happen

wednesday.question: What does 'definitely' mean?
options: Perhaps; it might happen
Probably; very likely
Without any doubt; certainly
Rarely; almost never
wednesday.correctAnswer: Without any doubt; certainly

wednesday.question: What does 'chance' mean in 'There's a good chance it will rain'?
options: A confirmed schedule
A possibility that something will happen
A warning about danger
A plan made for the future
wednesday.correctAnswer: A possibility that something will happen

wednesday.question: Which modal verb expresses the highest certainty about a future event?
options: might
could
may
will
wednesday.correctAnswer: will

wednesday.question: Which sentence correctly makes a future prediction?
options: It will probably rains tomorrow.
It will probably rain tomorrow.
It will probably rained tomorrow.
It will probably raining tomorrow.
wednesday.correctAnswer: It will probably rain tomorrow.

wednesday.question: What does 'She might come to the party' mean?
options: She will definitely come to the party.
She is at the party right now.
There is a possibility she will come, but it's not certain.
She has already been to the party.
wednesday.correctAnswer: There is a possibility she will come, but it's not certain.

wednesday.question: Where is the probability adverb correctly placed?
options: He definitely will pass the test.
He will pass definitely the test.
He will definitely pass the test.
Definitely he will pass the test.
wednesday.correctAnswer: He will definitely pass the test.

wednesday.question: Which sentence expresses the LEAST certainty about the future?
options: She will definitely be there.
She will probably be there.
She might be there.
She is going to be there.
wednesday.correctAnswer: She might be there.

wednesday.question: Complete: 'I think it _____ rain this afternoon — look at those dark clouds.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: might|may|will

wednesday.question: Complete: 'Scientists _____ that sea levels will rise significantly by 2100.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: predict

wednesday.question: Complete: 'There is a good _____ that the project will be finished on time.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: chance

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'Talvez eu vá à festa amanhã.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: i might go to the party tomorrow

wednesday.question: Translate to English: 'É provável que ele chegue atrasado.'
wednesday.correctAnswer: he will probably arrive late

thursday.question: What does 'debate' mean?
options: A casual conversation between friends
A formal discussion where people express and defend different viewpoints
A written summary of an argument
A type of question with no answer
thursday.correctAnswer: A formal discussion where people express and defend different viewpoints

thursday.question: What does 'absolutely' mean when used in a conversation?
options: Used to strongly agree or emphasize certainty
Used to express doubt or uncertainty
Used to introduce a new topic
Used to politely disagree
thursday.correctAnswer: Used to strongly agree or emphasize certainty

thursday.question: What does 'although' mean?
options: In addition to; also
As a result of; therefore
Despite the fact that; even though
For example; such as
thursday.correctAnswer: Despite the fact that; even though

thursday.question: What does 'whereas' mean?
options: Because of this; as a result
Used to contrast two different facts or situations
In the same way as
At the same time as
thursday.correctAnswer: Used to contrast two different facts or situations

thursday.question: Which sentence correctly uses Present Perfect with a life experience?
options: I have tried sushi last year.
I tried sushi in my life.
I have tried sushi before.
I am tried sushi before.

thursday.question: Which question correctly uses 'yet'?
options: Has she already finished yet?
Has she finished yet?
She has finished yet?
Did she finish yet?

thursday.question: Which phrase shows the STRONGEST disagreement?
options: I see your point, but...
I'm not entirely sure about that.
I completely disagree with that statement.
That's an interesting perspective.
thursday.correctAnswer: I completely disagree with that statement.

thursday.question: Which sentence correctly uses 'could' to express a future possibility?
options: It could rains tomorrow.
It could rain tomorrow.
It could rained tomorrow.
It could to rain tomorrow.

thursday.question: Which sentence uses 'may' correctly to express possibility?
options: She may to change her mind.
She mays change her mind.
She may change her mind.
She may changes her mind.

thursday.question: Which is the correct way to express a strong future prediction?
options: Technology might definitely change the world.
Technology will definitely change the world.
Technology could definitely will change the world.
Technology may will change the world.
thursday.correctAnswer: Technology will definitely change the world.

thursday.question: Complete: 'Have you _____ visited a country in Asia?'
Complete: 'I _____ already done my homework, so I can go out now.'
Complete: '_____ my perspective, working from home increases productivity.'
Complete: 'The economy _____ probably improve in the next few years.'
Complete: 'I strongly _____ with your view on climate change.'
'Have you seen this film?' — 'No, I _____. Is it good?'
'Do you agree with the new policy?' — 'Not entirely. _____ my opinion, it needs some changes.'
'Will she come to the meeting?' — 'She _____. She hasn't confirmed yet.'
Translate to English: 'Eu nunca experimentei comida indiana.'
thursday.correctAnswer: i have never tried indian food

thursday.question: Translate to English: 'Isso provavelmente vai mudar no futuro.'
thursday.correctAnswer: this will probably change in the future

friday.question: What does 'recently' mean in 'I have recently started a new job'?
options: A very long time ago
Not long ago; in the near past
At a specific time in history
At some point in the future
friday.correctAnswer: Not long ago; in the near past

friday.question: What does 'anticipate' mean?
options: To react to something after it happens
To be completely surprised by something
To think about and prepare for something expected to happen
To ignore something that is coming
friday.correctAnswer: To think about and prepare for something expected to happen

friday.question: What does 'convince' mean?
options: To agree with someone immediately
To cause someone to believe that something is true
To repeat the same argument several times
To change your own mind without reason
friday.correctAnswer: To cause someone to believe that something is true

friday.question: What does 'however' mean in a discussion context?
options: Used to add more information to the same point
Used to give an example
Used to introduce a contrast or opposite idea
Used to summarize the conclusion
friday.correctAnswer: Used to introduce a contrast or opposite idea

friday.question: What does 'unlikely' mean in 'It is unlikely to snow in July'?
options: It will certainly snow in July.
It is not expected to happen; it probably won't occur.
It might snow, and nobody knows.
It has snowed in July before.
friday.correctAnswer: It is not expected to happen; it probably won't occur.

friday.question: What does 'so far' mean in 'So far, the results have been positive'?
options: At a great physical distance
In the near future
Until now; up to this point in time
After a long time has passed
friday.correctAnswer: Until now; up to this point in time

friday.question: Which sentence uses 'just' correctly in Present Perfect?
options: I just have finished my lunch.
I have finished just my lunch.
I have just finished my lunch.
Just I have finished my lunch.
friday.correctAnswer: I have just finished my lunch.

friday.question: 'I have never been to Africa.' What does this tell us?
options: The speaker went to Africa once.
The speaker is currently in Africa.
The speaker has not visited Africa at any point in their life.
The speaker has plans to visit Africa soon.
friday.correctAnswer: The speaker has not visited Africa at any point in their life.

friday.question: Which is the most natural response when you agree with someone's opinion?
options: I don't think that's right.
Absolutely, I couldn't agree more.
That makes no sense to me.
I see it very differently.

friday.question: Complete the sentence: 'I see your _____, but I still think we should try a different approach.'
options: mind
idea
point
opinion

friday.question: Which sentence uses 'since' correctly with Present Perfect?
options: She has worked here since five years.
She has worked here since 2019.
She worked here since 2019.
She has work here since 2019.

friday.question: Which modal shows the LEAST certainty about the future?
options: will
probably will
might
is going to

friday.question: Complete: 'She has _____ arrived — she just walked through the door.'
Complete: 'It _____ be cold tonight — I'm not sure, but bring a jacket.'
Complete: 'He got the job! He _____ been trying for months.'
'Have you finished the project yet?' — 'Yes, I've _____ finished it. I did it this morning.'
'What do you think about remote work?' — '_____ my perspective, it gives people more freedom.'
'Will it rain tomorrow?' — 'It _____. The forecast is not very clear.'
Translate to English: 'Do meu ponto de vista, todos deveriam aprender um segundo idioma.'
friday.correctAnswer: from my point of view, everyone should learn a second language

friday.question: Translate to English: 'Pode ser que ela mude de ideia.'
